evidence_refs.quote: We present the Dual-Trace Memory Encoder, a memory-writing strategy that augments fact-only records with compact scene-anchor cues to improve temporal and contextual recall. In a deterministic synthetic writer-swap benchmark, an initial dual-trace writer achieved a +48.6 percentage-point accuracy gain over a fact-only baseline (77.7% vs. 29.1%) but violated a storage-overhead constraint (+108.8% overhead vs. the ≤10% target). A compressed variant that retains event facts plus a single scene cue
